mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate profits and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable consumer prices indicates robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, supporting corporate revenue expansion and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving supply-side capacity utilization, which reduces input bottlenecks and boosts short-term equity momentum, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, with a hit rate below 45% or a negative cumulative alpha over 12-month rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which directly supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward returns, or the signal Rank IC drops below 0.02 across multiple 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 250.0, elevated inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, triggering immediate risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 250.0 are followed by positive 5-day returns in >50% of cases, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside expanding industrial production indicate synchronized strength across residential and manufacturing sectors, confirming broad-based economic momentum and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST and INDPRO signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over individual series in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth signals weakening aggregate demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and triggers portfolio de-risking, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 declines are followed by positive 60-day returns in >60% of instances during late-cycle phases, or the signal fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample backtests.
